title: Podman is not monitoring slirp4netns
body:
/kind bug **Description** Podman is not monitoring the `slirp4netns` process and if it dies, no one restarts the process and the network (eg host-port) communication is clearly broken. Please apologize if Podman is the wrong place to file this issue, I am not that familiar with this project. :) **Steps to reproduce the issue:** ``` m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ podman run -d --name nginx -p 33333:80 nginx adf9356c73cfc4d4b5db6db1ba7f0705ec5ec0d225dbf4e5bc51aad20c9c9962 m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ curl localhost:33333 Welcome to nginx!

Welcome to nginx!

If you see this page, the nginx web server is successfully installed and working. Further configuration is required.

For online documentation and support please refer to nginx.org.
Commercial support is available at nginx.com.

Thank you for using nginx.

m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ netstat -ntulp | grep 33333 (Not all processes could be identified, non-owned process info will not be shown, you would have to be root to see it all.) tcp 0 0 0.0.0.0:33333 0.0.0.0:* LISTEN 10763/slirp4netns m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ kill 10763 m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ netstat -ntulp | grep 33333 (Not all processes could be identified, non-owned process info will not be shown, you would have to be root to see it all.) m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ curl localhost:33333 curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 33333: Connection refused m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ podman ps CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES adf9356c73cf docker.io/library/nginx:latest nginx -g daemon o... 46 seconds ago Up 45 seconds ago 0.0.0.0:33333->80/tcp nginx m4r10k@fatal-error:~$ ``` **Describe the results you received:** Podman should take care about the `slirp4netns` process because if it dies, no one restarts the process and the network (eg host-port) communication is clearly broken. **Describe the results you expected:** If the `slirp4netns` process of a pod dies, it should be restarted like a container of the pod. **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 1.6.2 RemoteAPI Version: 1 Go Version: go1.10.4 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` debug: compiler: gc git commit: "" go version: go1.10.4 podman version: 1.6.2 host: BuildahVersion: 1.11.3 CgroupVersion: v1 Conmon: package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon' path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.2, commit: unknown' Distribution: distribution: ubuntu version: "19.10" IDMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 MemFree: 8701878272 MemTotal: 16790765568 OCIRuntime: name: runc package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc' path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev' SwapFree: 2147479552 SwapTotal: 2147479552 arch: amd64 cpus: 8 eventlogger: journald hostname: fatal-error kernel: 5.3.0-19-generic os: linux rootless: true slirp4netns: Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns Package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns' Version: |- slirp4netns version 0.4.2 commit: unknown uptime: 47m 42.91s registries: blocked: null insecure: null search: - docker.io - registry.fedoraproject.org - registry.access.redhat.com store: ConfigFile: /home/m4r10k/.config/containers/storage.conf ContainerStore: number: 4 GraphDriverName: vfs GraphOptions: {} GraphRoot: /home/m4r10k/.local/share/containers/storage GraphStatus: {} ImageStore: number: 2 RunRoot: /run/user/1000 VolumePath: /home/m4r10k/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ``` Listing... Done podman/now 1.6.2-1~ubuntu19.04~ppa1 amd64 [installed,local] ```

comments.body:
yes, that is out of the scope.  We don't want to leave a podman process alive to monitor the slirp4netns process.  We could probably add this logic to the slirp4netns process itself to have a watchdog process.  @AkihiroSuda what do you think?

How did the slirp4netns process fail?  Was it killed?

@giuseppe @AkihiroSuda Uhm, I killed it with full intend to find out what happens if it happen :see_no_evil: :smile: 

My guess about Podman as monitoring instance was that it already has to monitor the Pod containers (failing/restart policies/...) and it has to use slirp4netns to setup the network TAP networking to provide the ip packet forwarding service between the host network and the Pod network namespace/cidr. But thats just my small point of view of course :see_no_evil:
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
